findings.evidence: Line 81 LHS is `legendreSym q D`; the odd parallel `legendreSym_oddPrimeDiscriminant_eq_legendreSym` is keyed on `legendreSym q (oddPrimeDiscriminant p)` (LegendrePrimeDiscriminant.lean:63), and the radicand generic at line 140 keys on the recognizable head `evenPrimeDiscriminantRadicand D`.
findings.fix: Drop `@[simp]`, keeping it a plain characteristic lemma. The literal-keyed `legendreSym_evenPrimeDiscriminant_{neg_four,eight,neg_eight}_eq_one_iff` (lines 166/173/180) already give simp well-targeted coverage.
findings.issue: `@[simp]` on `legendreSym_evenPrimeDiscriminant_eq_legendreSym_radicand` keys on `legendreSym q D` with `D` a bare variable, so simp attempts it (and its hypothesis discharge) on every Legendre symbol; it also rewrites the prime discriminant into its radicand, the opposite direction from the stated genus-field use where `D` is the splitting character.

findings.evidence: Line 155 LHS `legendreSym q D = 1`; lines 166/173/180 already prove `legendreSym q (-4)/8/(-8) = 1 ↔ ...` and carry `@[simp]`.
findings.fix: Drop `@[simp]`; keep it as the plain generic characteristic lemma the three concrete simp lemmas are derived from.
findings.issue: `@[simp]` on `legendreSym_evenPrimeDiscriminant_eq_one_iff` has the same bare `legendreSym q D = 1` key and is redundant with the literal-keyed simp lemmas at lines 166/173/180.

summary: The prior missing-@[simp] findings are resolved and the characteristic API is complete with a minimal surface and a private helper. But two of the added @[simp] lemmas are keyed on a bare-variable Legendre application, making them over-broad and redundant with the literal-keyed forms.
